meaning: n.性感,性的魅力,精神,精力
examples: "There's no buzz, there's no oomph about the place," he complained.
Marley just did not have the oomph needed to make it to the top.
They have more oomph, and no need of a gearbox to deliver it.
Vytorin combines Zocor with a newer drug called Zetia that adds additional cholesterol-lowering oomph.
On the one hand, this would certainly put a lot of oomph behind the iPhone.
